perturbation.original_text: Article 3 "Articles of Association" means the Articles of Association of the Joint Venture Company signed by Party A and Party B simultaneously with this Contract in Baoding, People's Republic of China.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 3 "Articles of Association" means a document that may or may not be fully executed or signed by Party A and Party B. Its existence is dependent on future negotiations, separate from this contract.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the 'Articles of Association' as a document signed simultaneously with the contract, establishing a firm agreement from the start. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating the document's existence depends on future negotiations, potentially contradicting the initial assertion of its simultaneous creation and agreed-upon nature. The term 'Articles of Association' is now open to interpretation, and uncertain in its application.
perturbation.contradicted_location: CHAPTER 5 THE PURPOSE, SCOPE AND SCALE OF PRODUCTION AND BUSINESS - Article 27
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 27 The purpose of the Joint Venture Company is to use advanced technology and scientific management techniques to produce and sell the Joint Venture Products, to improve the quality and increase the value and competitiveness of such products, to develop and introduce new products and to obtain satisfactory economic benefits for the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text firmly establishes the 'Articles of Association' as a simultaneously signed document, which implies its existence and agreement on its terms. The changed text introduces uncertainty by suggesting the 'Articles of Association' may not be fully executed or signed and is dependent on future negotiations. This directly contradicts Article 27, which outlines the purpose of the Joint Venture Company, as the company's operational basis and objectives are predicated on a defined and agreed-upon set of 'Articles of Association'. Without a firmly established 'Articles of Association', as described in the original text, the purpose outlined in Article 27 becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable because the foundation upon which the joint venture company is meant to operate becomes uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: Article 66 The CEO shall be nominated by Party B, and the Deputy CEO shall be nominated by Party A The Chief Financial Officer shall be nominated by Party A, and an Assistant Financial Officer may be nominated by Party B. The Chief Financial Officer must consult with the Assistant Financial Officer, if any, on all important financial matters. Each officer nominated by a party or parties in accordance with this paragraph shall be appointed by the Board of Directors. The other Management Personnel shall be nominated by the CEO and appointed by the Board of Directors. The Board may dismiss any Management Personnel. All replacements for any of the Management Personnel, whether by reason of the retirement, resignation, disability or death of a manager or of the removal of a manager by the Board of Directors or by the Party which nominated him, shall be nominated and appointed in the same manner as the original appointee. Other details of management shall be decided by the CEO.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 66 The CEO shall be nominated by Party B, and the Deputy CEO shall be nominated by Party A. The Chief Financial Officer shall be nominated by Party A, and an Assistant Financial Officer may be nominated by Party B. The Chief Financial Officer is not obligated to consult the Assistant Financial Officer. Each officer nominated by a party or parties in accordance with this paragraph shall be appointed by the Board of Directors. The other Management Personnel shall be nominated by the CEO and appointed by the Board of Directors. The Board may dismiss any Management Personnel. All replacements for any of the Management Personnel, whether by reason of the retirement, resignation, disability or death of a manager or of the removal of a manager by the Board of Directors or by the Party which nominated him, shall be nominated and appointed in the same manner as the original appointee. Other details of management shall be decided by the CEO.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that 'The Chief Financial Officer must consult with the Assistant Financial Officer, if any, on all important financial matters.' By removing this obligation, and stating 'The Chief Financial Officer is not obligated to consult the Assistant Financial Officer' it creates a direct contradiction and could lead to disputes and creates uncertainty regarding the authority of each position.
perturbation.contradicted_location: CHAPTER 15 FINANCIAL AFFAIRS AND ACCOUNTING - Article 82
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 82 The Chief Financial Officer and Assistant Financial Officer of the Joint Venture Company, under the leadership of the CEO, shall be responsible for the financial management of the Joint Venture Company. The CEO, the Chief Financial Officer and Assistant Financial Officer shall prepare the Joint Venture Company's accounting system and procedures in accordance with the relevant PRC laws and regulations, and submit the same to the Board of Directors for adoption.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the mandatory consultation between the Chief Financial Officer and the Assistant Financial Officer, stating 'The Chief Financial Officer is not obligated to consult the Assistant Financial Officer.' This directly contradicts Article 82, which states that 'The Chief Financial Officer and Assistant Financial Officer of the Joint Venture Company, under the leadership of the CEO, shall be responsible for the financial management of the Joint Venture Company.' By removing the consultation obligation, it undermines the shared responsibility and creates a strong conflict regarding the roles and responsibilities of the CFO and Assistant CFO as defined in Article 82.

perturbation.original_text: Article 104 Each Party shall have the right to terminate this Contract prior to the expiration of the Contract Term by written notice to the other Party if any of the following events occur: (1) in the event that either party fails to make its capital contribution, in whole or in part, within [90] days of the due date, or in the event that any of the conditions precedent set forth in Article 34 of this Contract have not been satisfied or waived within [120] days of the date on which this Contract is signed by the parties; (2) the other Party materially breaches this Contract or materially violates the Articles of Association, and such breach or violation is not cured within sixty (60) days of written notice to the breaching/violating Party; (3) the Joint Venture Company or the other Party becomes bankrupt, or is the subject of proceedings for liquidation or dissolution, or ceases to carry on business, or becomes unable to pay its debts as they come due; (4) the other Party transfers all or any part of its share of the Joint Venture Company's registered capital in violation of the provisions of this Contract; (5) any government authority having authority over any Party requires any provision of this Contract or the Articles of Association to be revised in such a way as to cause significant adverse consequences to the Joint Venture Company or any Party; (6) the conditions or consequences of Force Majeure prevail with the result of a major impairment to the functioning of the Joint Venture Company for a period in excess of six (6) months and the Parties have been unable to find an equitable solution; or (7) the Parties cannot implement the economic adjustment described in Article 119.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 104 Each Party shall have the right to terminate this Contract prior to the expiration of the Contract Term without written notice to the other Party if any of the following events occur: (1) in the event that either party fails to make its capital contribution, in whole or in part, within [90] days of the due date, or in the event that any of the conditions precedent set forth in Article 34 of this Contract have not been satisfied or waived within [120] days of the date on which this Contract is signed by the parties; (2) the other Party materially breaches this Contract or materially violates the Articles of Association, and such breach or violation is not cured within sixty (60) days of written notice to the breaching/violating Party; (3) the Joint Venture Company or the other Party becomes bankrupt, or is the subject of proceedings for liquidation or dissolution, or ceases to carry on business, or becomes unable to pay its debts as they come due; (4) the other Party transfers all or any part of its share of the Joint Venture Company's registered capital in violation of the provisions of this Contract; (5) any government authority having authority over any Party requires any provision of this Contract or the Articles of Association to be revised in such a way as to cause significant adverse consequences to the Joint Venture Company or any Party; (6) the conditions or consequences of Force Majeure prevail with the result of a major impairment to the functioning of the Joint Venture Company for a period in excess of six (6) months and the Parties have been unable to find an equitable solution; or (7) the Parties cannot implement the economic adjustment described in Article 119.
perturbation.explanation: The original text required a 'written notice' before terminating the contract. By removing the 'written notice' portion, it causes a contradiction since it gives the option to any part to teminate the contract without notice. The absence of notice can cause uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 116 In the event a dispute arises in connection with the interpretation or implementation of this Contract, the Parties shall attempt in the first instance to resolve such dispute through friendly consultations. If the dispute is not resolved through consultations within sixty (60) days after one Party has served a written notice on the other Party requesting the commencement of consultations, then any Party may refer the dispute to arbitration in Singapore under the auspices of the Singapore International Arbitration Centre in accordance with the rules of that Centre for the time being in force and the provisions of Article 117 of this Contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement of 'written notice' for contract termination, directly contradicting Article 116, which mandates a 'written notice' as a prerequisite for initiating dispute resolution through consultation. The original text aligns with Article 116, requiring written notice before termination, which preserves the possibility of consultation before arbitration as outlined in Article 116. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
